div1.entryFree:
Glorify, 1) to make glorious, to give splendor to: the bright sun --es the sky, Ven. 485. such silver currents do g. the banks that bound them in, John II, 442. I will not return till my attempt so much be --ed as to my ample hope was promised, V, 2, 111. death's dishonourable victory we with our stately presence g. H6A I, 1, 21.
2) to pay praise, to magnify in worship: that we for thee may g. the Lord, H6B II, 1, 75.
